action.reasoning: Observation: The best test balanced_accuracy remains 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt). Multiple recent attempts (3-way ensembles, tau-normalization, cRT, focal loss, new seeds) have failed to improve. Analysis: The base ConvNeXt-Tiny (best_iter9.pt) is the strongest anchor. Instead of training a new backbone from scratch, I will fine-tune this existing checkpoint with stronger regularization (MixUp α=0.2, RandomErasing p=0.25, RandAugment magnitude 15, RandomResizedCrop) and a lower learning rate (backbone 5e-5, head 5e-4) for 15 epochs. This should nudge the model to a broader minimum with different error patterns, especially for tail classes, while preserving the strong head-class features. After fine-tuning, I will immediately run a TTA-aware ensemble grid search between the original best_iter9.pt and the new fine-tuned model, then submit the resulting ensemble. This is fast (~6-7 min total) and low-risk because the ensemble can down-weight the new model if it underperforms.
action.command: python finetune.py --epochs 15 --output checkpoints/ft.pt && python tune_ft.py --ckpt2 checkpoints/ft.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_ft.pt
